notes: Batons are not outright banned in Canada, but carrying one for self-defence can make it an illegal weapon.
Kubotans are generally legal as everyday items but carrying one as a weapon can make it illegal
Self-defence keychains may be legal if they are ordinary items, but weaponised versions can be treated as prohibited weapons.
Carrying a sword in public is legal in Canada only if there is a legitimate reason and no intent to use it as a weapon.
Tasers are prohibited weapons in Canada and are not legal for civilian ownership.
Pepper spray for use against humans is generally prohibited in Canada
Switchblades are prohibited in Canada and cannot be legally owned or imported.
Butterfly knives are considered prohibited weapons in Canada and are illegal to possess.
Stun guns are prohibited weapons in Canada and cannot be legally possessed by civilians.
Brass knuckles are prohibited weapons in Canada and cannot be legally owned or carried.
